design_philosophy.core_loop: Select puzzle-solving approach using point-and-click interface; navigate mansion avoiding hostile NPCs; solve character-specific puzzles to progress toward rescuing Sandy
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Exploration risk—hostile mansion inhabitants will capture or kill player characters if discovered; character loss is permanent until game over
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Teen detective/adventurer infiltrating dangerous mansion to rescue girlfriend; empowerment through character selection and creative problem-solving
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Non-linear puzzle design where multiple solutions exist based on party composition; story branches into five distinct endings; point-and-click interface chosen explicitly to reject command-line adventure conventions; comedic tone subverts horror B-movie tropes

difficulty.lives_system: Three characters maximum; when a character is caught by NPCs or killed, they are permanently lost. Game ends when all three characters are eliminated
difficulty.checkpointing: No explicit checkpoint system documented; player must manage risk of character death through strategic navigation
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection at start determines available puzzle solutions and party capabilities; player chooses which paths to take and which characters to risk in dangerous situations

progression.unlock_mechanism: Character-gated puzzles—different character abilities (e.g., Syd/Razor can play instruments, Bernard can repair appliances) unlock different solution paths; exploration of mansion reveals new areas and puzzle opportunities
replayability_hooks: Five possible endings based on character selection and survival
Multiple puzzle solutions per scenario based on party composition
Six playable characters to recruit in different pairs creates 15 unique party combinations
Non-linear mansion layout encourages different exploration routes

world_building_techniques: Intro cutscene establishes 20-year history of sentient meteor, brainwashed Edisons, and kidnapping plot
Environmental storytelling through mansion layout and NPC dialogue
Cutscenes interrupt gameplay to advance story and convey offscreen events
Character dialogue reveals personality and motivations through point-and-click interactions

faction_indicators: Edison family (hostile): Dr. Fred (mad scientist), Nurse Edna (wife), Weird Ed (son)
Green tentacle (ally): sympathetic to player characters
Purple tentacle (hostile): enslaved by sentient meteor
danger_signals: Mansion inhabitants actively hunt player characters and throw them into dungeon
Character death is permanent within a run; NPC patrols create exploration tension
Sentient meteor intelligence drives antagonist motivations
environmental_storytelling: Mansion rooms decorated to reflect character personalities and scientific experiments
Visual design of Edison family home reflects mad scientist aesthetic
Presence of disembodied tentacles creates surreal, unsettling atmosphere
